---
title: "Samiran Dutta"
---

::: {.layout-grid}

::: {.left-col}
<div class="name">Samiran Dutta</div>

![](images/headshot.jpg){.headshot}

<div class="left-title">
PhD Candidate in Economics<br>
Paris School of Economics
</div>

<div class="left-email">
<a href="mailto:samiran.dutta@psemail.eu"><span class="email-label">samiran.dutta@psemail.eu</span></a>
</div>

<div class="left-links">
<a href="files/cv.pdf" class="cv-bracket" target="_blank">&lt;&nbsp;CV&nbsp;&gt;</a>
<span class="social-bracket">&lt;&nbsp;<a href="https://github.com/samirandutta" target="_blank" rel="noopener" aria-label="GitHub" class="social-icon-link"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="15" height="15" fill="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 16 16"><path d="M8 0C3.58 0 0 3.58 0 8c0 3.54 2.29 6.53 5.47 7.59.4.07.55-.17.55-.38 0-.19-.01-.82-.01-1.49-2.01.37-2.53-.49-2.69-.94-.09-.23-.48-.94-.82-1.13-.28-.15-.68-.52-.01-.53.63-.01 1.08.58 1.23.82.72 1.21 1.87.87 2.33.66.07-.52.28-.87.51-1.07-1.78-.2-3.64-.89-3.64-3.95 0-.87.31-1.59.82-2.15-.08-.2-.36-1.02.08-2.12 0 0 .67-.21 2.2.82.64-.18 1.32-.27 2-.27s1.36.09 2 .27c1.53-1.04 2.2-.82 2.2-.82.44 1.1.16 1.92.08 2.12.51.56.82 1.27.82 2.15 0 3.07-1.87 3.75-3.65 3.95.29.25.54.73.54 1.48 0 1.07-.01 1.93-.01 2.2 0 .21.15.46.55.38A8.01 8.01 0 0 0 16 8c0-4.42-3.58-8-8-8z"/></svg></a>&nbsp;<a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=Nul09cIAAAAJ&hl=en" target="_blank" rel="noopener" aria-label="Google Scholar" class="social-icon-link"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="15" height="15" fill="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path d="M5.242 13.769 0 9.5 12 0l12 9.5-5.242 4.269C17.548 11.249 14.978 9.5 12 9.5c-2.977 0-5.548 1.748-6.758 4.269zM12 10a7 7 0 1 0 0 14 7 7 0 0 0 0-14z"/></svg></a>&nbsp;<a href="https://x.com/samiran_dutta19" target="_blank" rel="noopener" aria-label="Twitter/X" class="social-icon-link"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="14" height="14" fill="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path d="M18.244 2.25h3.308l-7.227 8.26 8.502 11.24H16.17l-5.214-6.817L4.99 21.75H1.68l7.73-8.835L1.254 2.25H8.08l4.713 6.231zm-1.161 17.52h1.833L7.084 4.126H5.117z"/></svg></a>&nbsp;&gt;</span>
</div>

:::

::: {.right-col}

<div class="top-nav">
<a href="#working-papers"><span class="nav-label">Working Papers</span></a>
<a href="#work-in-progress"><span class="nav-label">Work in Progress</span></a>
<a href="#publications"><span class="nav-label">Publications</span></a>
<a href="#teaching"><span class="nav-label">Teaching</span></a>
<a href="#code"><span class="nav-label">Code</span></a>
</div>

I am a PhD candidate in Economics at the Paris School of Economics. My research lies at the intersection of macroeconomics and labor economics, with a focus on firm dynamics, labor market frictions, and multi-worker wage bargaining. In Spring 2025, I was a visiting scholar at the University of Edinburgh, hosted by [Prof. Mike Elsby](https://sites.google.com/site/mikeelsby/){target="_blank"} and [Prof. Axel Gottfries](https://sites.google.com/site/axelgottfries/){target="_blank"}.

Prior to my doctoral studies, I earned an MRes in Economics from the Paris School of Economics and a B.A. (Hons.) from University of Delhi. I was previously affiliated with the India-KLEMS project at the Centre for Development Economics, Delhi School of Economics, where I worked on the production of the [India Productivity Report](https://rbi.org.in/Scripts/PublicationReportDetails.aspx?UrlPage=&ID=1217){target="_blank"} in collaboration with the Reserve Bank of India.

## WORK IN PROGRESS {#work-in-progress}

<div class="paper">
<div class="paper-title">
The Reserve Army of Labor: Firm Dynamics and Dual Labor Markets in India
</div>
<div class="paper-buttons">
<button class="toggle-btn" onclick="togglePanel(this,'ra-abstract')"><span class="btn-label">Abstract</span></button>
<button class="toggle-btn" onclick="togglePanel(this,'ra-presentations')"><span class="btn-label">Presentations</span></button>
<button class="toggle-btn" onclick="togglePanel(this,'ra-funding')"><span class="btn-label">Funding</span></button>
</div>
<div id="ra-abstract" class="toggle-panel">This paper develops a search-and-matching model of large firms with productivity heterogeneity, dual labor markets, and unionized wage bargaining. Firms hire through two channels — a regular labor market with matching frictions, adjustment costs, and collective bargaining, and a contract labor market where contractors supply workers for a per-head fee without frictions. Adjustment costs generate inaction regions in regular employment — consistent with novel establishment-level evidence from India's manufacturing sector — while contract labor provides a flexible margin to absorb productivity shocks. Larger firms strategically "overemploy" contract workers, using them not only as a substitute input but also to exert downward pressure on union-negotiated regular worker wages. The model replicates key empirical patterns in India's manufacturing sector, where contract workers function as a de facto "reserve" workforce, offering flexibility and surplus value through wage compression. The framework highlights an output–wage trade-off — contract labor reduces misallocation by easing adjustment but simultaneously weakens collective bargaining, compressing regular wages.</div>
<div id="ra-presentations" class="toggle-panel">Macro Workshop, PSE (May 2024, October 2025); Macro Reading Group, University of Edinburgh (April 2025); Labor/Public Reading Group, Sciences Po (October 2025); ACEGD, ISI Delhi (December 2025)</div>
<div id="ra-funding" class="toggle-panel">EUR PgSE.</div>
</div>

<div class="paper">
<div class="paper-title">
Unions, Hold-up, and Joint Dynamics of Labor and Capital
</div>
<div class="paper-coauthors">
with Alex McQuitty
</div>
</div>

<div class="paper">
<div class="paper-title">
Public Infrastructure and Intra-Sector Misallocation: Theory and Evidence from India
</div>
<div class="paper-coauthors">
with François Fontaine and Sarath C. Mudigonda
</div>
</div>

## WORKING PAPERS {#working-papers}

<div class="paper">
<div class="paper-title">
Investigating the Prevalence of 'Missing Middle' in Indian Manufacturing
</div>
<div class="paper-coauthors">with B. N. Goldar and P. Majumder
</div>
<div class="paper-buttons">
<button class="toggle-btn" onclick="togglePanel(this,'mm-abstract')"><span class="btn-label">Abstract</span></button>
</div>
<div id="mm-abstract" class="toggle-panel">This paper re-examines the "missing middle" in Indian manufacturing using unit-level data from the NSS 73rd Round and the ASI for 2015–16, covering unorganized and organized segments, respectively. Moving beyond size-class employment shares, we adopt a Pareto-based empirical strategy to assess deviations in the firm-size distribution. The findings indicate evidence consistent with a missing middle, further supported by variations in survival probabilities across size classes. </div>
</div>

## PUBLICATIONS {#publications}

<div class="paper">
<div class="paper-title">
The Role of Services in India's Post-Reform Economic Growth
</div>
<div class="paper-coauthors">with B. N. Goldar and P. C. Das — *Structural Change and Economic Dynamics*, 2024
</div>
<div class="paper-buttons">
<button class="toggle-btn" onclick="togglePanel(this,'services-abstract')"><span class="btn-label">Abstract</span></button>
<button class="toggle-btn" onclick="togglePanel(this,'services-presentations')"><span class="btn-label">Presentations</span></button>
</div>
<div id="services-abstract" class="toggle-panel">Leveraging industry-level data from the India KLEMS database for 1993–2018, we trace how services became India's principal growth driver after the 1990s reforms. Over the period, the sector's value-added share rose from 41% to 53% and real output grew 7.5 % per year — roughly half of aggregate GDP growth. We estimate total factor productivity (TFP) for 14 service industries and decompose their contributions to sector-wide TFP. Transport & storage emerges as the single largest contributor among market services, followed by financial services; in non-market activities, public administration dominates. Panel regressions reveal strong positive spillovers from manufacturing TFP to both market and non-market services, while an augmented model with foreign-sector variables confirms significant productivity diffusion from advanced economies. Taken together, the results highlight inter-sectoral and international linkages as critical channels sustaining India's service-led growth.</div>
<div id="services-presentations" class="toggle-panel">Seventh World KLEMS Conference, University of Manchester (virtual)</div>
</div>

## TEACHING {#teaching}

<div class="teaching-group">

- **Research methods in Econometrics** — Masters in Economics and Psychology, PSE (2026)
- **Introduction to Econometrics** — Masters in Economics and Psychology, PSE (2024, 2025)
- **International Trade** —  Undergraduate L3, Université Paris 1 Pantheón-Sorbonne (2024)
- **Macroeconomics - Growth Theory** — Masters, QEM, Université Paris 1 Pantheón-Sorbonne (2023)

</div>

## CODE {#code}

<div class="code-entry">

### [**> Macro-Labor Models**](https://github.com/samirandutta/macro_labor_models){target="_blank"}

The repository contains JULIA code for replicating the main results of seminal models in macro-labor. These are updated as I progress through my own learning. 

<div class="model-cards">
<div class="model-card">
<button class="toggle-btn" onclick="togglePanel(this,'model-hop92')"><span class="btn-label"> Hopenhayn (1992)</span></button>
<div id="model-hop92" class="toggle-panel">Industry equilibrium with heterogeneous firms, entry/exit, and stationary firm-size distributions. Solves for the value function, stationary distribution of firms, and equilibrium entry/exit cutoffs.
<div class="model-figures">
<img src="images/hop_value.png" alt="Value function">
<img src="images/hop_dist.png" alt="Firm size distribution">
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="model-card">
<button class="toggle-btn" onclick="togglePanel(this,'model-hopreg93')"><span class="btn-label"> Hopenhayn and Rogerson (1993)</span></button>
<div id="model-hopreg93" class="toggle-panel">General equilibrium with firing costs and labor misallocation. Solves for the value function, stationary distribution of firms, equilibrium cutoff and inaction thresholds. 
<div class="model-figures">
<img src="images/hr_value_function.png" alt="Value function">
<img src="images/hr_inaction_region.png" alt="Inaction region">
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="model-card">
<button class="toggle-btn" onclick="togglePanel(this,'model-berg22')"><span class="btn-label"> Berger, Herkenhoff and Mongey (2022)</span></button>
<div id="model-berg22" class="toggle-panel">Labor market power with monopsonistic firms. Computes monopsony wedges, markdown on wages, and the aggregate output and welfare effects of market concentration.
<div class="model-figures">
<img src="images/mkdown.png" alt="Markdown on wages">
<img src="images/wbshare.png" alt="Wage bill share">
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

</div>


:::

:::

<script>
// Quarto's Safari scrollbar hack calls window.scrollTo(0,1) synchronously
// and window.scrollTo(0,0) again after 40ms, causing a jump to top.
// Suppress scrollTo entirely for 50ms so the page never moves during toggle.
(function() {
  const orig = window.quartoToggleColorScheme;
  if (orig) {
    window.quartoToggleColorScheme = function() {
      const scrollY = window.scrollY;
      const realScrollTo = window.scrollTo.bind(window);
      document.body.style.setProperty('display', 'block', 'important'); // prevent flex flash
      window.scrollTo = () => {};          // block all scrollTo calls
      orig();
      setTimeout(() => {
        window.scrollTo = realScrollTo;    // restore after Quarto's 40ms fires
        realScrollTo(0, scrollY);          // snap back to where we were
      }, 50);
    };
  }
})();

function togglePanel(btn, id) {
  const panel = document.getElementById(id);
  const isOpen = panel.style.display === 'block';
  panel.style.display = isOpen ? 'none' : 'block';
  btn.classList.toggle('active', !isOpen);
}

function applyDarkMode() {
  document.body.style.setProperty('display', 'block', 'important'); // beat any Bootstrap flex rule
  const isDark = document.body.classList.contains('quarto-dark');
  // Inject SVG moon (light mode) or sun (dark mode) into the toggle button
  const moonSVG = '<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="16" height="16" fill="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 16 16"><path d="M6 .278a.77.77 0 0 1 .08.858 7.2 7.2 0 0 0-.878 3.46c0 4.021 3.278 7.277 7.318 7.277q.792-.001 1.533-.16a.79.79 0 0 1 .81.316.73.73 0 0 1-.031.893A8.35 8.35 0 0 1 8.344 16C3.734 16 0 12.286 0 7.71 0 4.266 2.114 1.312 5.124.06A.75.75 0 0 1 6 .278"/></svg>';
  const sunSVG = '<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="16" height="16" fill="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 16 16"><path d="M8 11a3 3 0 1 1 0-6 3 3 0 0 1 0 6m0 1a4 4 0 1 0 0-8 4 4 0 0 0 0 8M8 0a.5.5 0 0 1 .5.5v2a.5.5 0 0 1-1 0v-2A.5.5 0 0 1 8 0m0 13a.5.5 0 0 1 .5.5v2a.5.5 0 0 1-1 0v-2A.5.5 0 0 1 8 13m8-5a.5.5 0 0 1-.5.5h-2a.5.5 0 0 1 0-1h2a.5.5 0 0 1 .5.5M3 8a.5.5 0 0 1-.5.5h-2a.5.5 0 0 1 0-1h2A.5.5 0 0 1 3 8m10.657-5.657a.5.5 0 0 1 0 .707l-1.414 1.415a.5.5 0 1 1-.707-.708l1.414-1.414a.5.5 0 0 1 .707 0m-9.193 9.193a.5.5 0 0 1 0 .707L3.05 13.657a.5.5 0 0 1-.707-.707l1.414-1.414a.5.5 0 0 1 .707 0m9.193 2.121a.5.5 0 0 1-.707 0l-1.414-1.414a.5.5 0 0 1 .707-.707l1.414 1.414a.5.5 0 0 1 0 .707M4.464 4.465a.5.5 0 0 1-.707 0L2.343 3.05a.5.5 0 1 1 .707-.707l1.414 1.414a.5.5 0 0 1 0 .707"/></svg>';
  document.querySelectorAll('.quarto-color-scheme-toggle').forEach(btn => {
    btn.innerHTML = isDark ? sunSVG : moonSVG;
  });
  // html background (Safari :has() bug workaround)
  document.documentElement.style.setProperty('background-color', isDark ? '#0c0c0c' : '', 'important');
  // Model cards and panels — Safari doesn't animate CSS-variable-driven transitions
  document.querySelectorAll('.model-card').forEach(el => {
    el.style.setProperty('background-color', isDark ? '#161616' : '#f7f7f7', 'important');
  });
  document.querySelectorAll('.toggle-panel').forEach(el => {
    el.style.setProperty('background-color', isDark ? '#161616' : '', 'important');
  });
  document.querySelectorAll('.right-col h2').forEach(el => {
    el.style.setProperty('color', isDark ? '#f0efe9' : '', 'important');
    el.style.setProperty('border-bottom-color', isDark ? '#555' : '', 'important');
  });
  document.querySelectorAll('hr').forEach(el => {
    el.style.setProperty('border-top-color', isDark ? '#555' : '', 'important');
  });
}

new MutationObserver(applyDarkMode)
  .observe(document.body, { attributes: true, attributeFilter: ['class'] });

// Run after Quarto's DOMContentLoaded creates the toggle <i> element
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => setTimeout(applyDarkMode, 0));
</script>
